hypothesis: For all natural numbers n, n plus zero equals n
proof:
add-zero : ∀ n → n + 0 ≡ n
add-zero zero = refl
add-zero (suc n) = cong suc (add-zero n)

hypothesis: For all natural numbers a and b, a plus b equals b plus a
proof:
add-comm : ∀ a b → a + b ≡ b + a
add-comm zero b = sym (+-identityʳ b)
add-comm (suc a) b = begin
  suc a + b   ≡⟨ refl ⟩
  suc (a + b) ≡⟨ cong suc (add-comm a b) ⟩
  suc (b + a) ≡⟨ sym (+-suc b a) ⟩
  b + suc a   ∎

hypothesis: For all lists, appending an empty list returns the original list
proof:
append-nil : ∀ {A : Set} (xs : List A) → xs ++ [] ≡ xs
append-nil [] = refl
append-nil (x ∷ xs) = cong (x ∷_) (append-nil xs)

hypothesis: If a number is divisible by 6, it is divisible by 2
proof:
div6-div2 : ∀ n → n mod 6 ≡ 0 → n mod 2 ≡ 0
div6-div2 n h = begin
  n mod 2 ≡⟨ mod-cong n (m*n≡0⇒m≡0 3 (begin
    3 * (n mod 2) ≡⟨ *-distribˡ-mod 3 n 2 ⟩
    (3 * n) mod 6 ≡⟨ cong (_mod 6) (sym (*-comm n 3)) ⟩
    (n * 3) mod 6 ≡⟨ *-mod-cong n 3 6 h ⟩
    0 ∎)) ⟩
  0 ∎

hypothesis: For all booleans b, not not b equals b
proof:
not-not : ∀ b → not (not b) ≡ b
not-not true = refl
not-not false = refl

hypothesis: For all natural numbers a, a times one equals a
proof:
*-identityʳ : ∀ a → a * 1 ≡ a
*-identityʳ zero = refl
*-identityʳ (suc a) = cong suc (*-identityʳ a)

hypothesis: If a is less than b and b is less than c, then a is less than c
proof:
<-trans : ∀ {a b c} → a < b → b < c → a < c
<-trans a<b b<c = ≤-trans a<b (≤-trans (s≤s (≤-reflexive refl)) b<c)

hypothesis: Zero is the left identity for addition
proof:
+-identityˡ : ∀ n → 0 + n ≡ n
+-identityˡ n = refl

hypothesis: For all natural numbers n, n times zero equals zero
proof:
*-zeroʳ : ∀ n → n * 0 ≡ 0
*-zeroʳ zero = refl
*-zeroʳ (suc n) = *-zeroʳ n

hypothesis: For all lists l, reversing l twice gives back l
proof:
reverse-involutive : ∀ {A : Set} (xs : List A) → reverse (reverse xs) ≡ xs
reverse-involutive [] = refl
reverse-involutive (x ∷ xs) = begin
  reverse (reverse (x ∷ xs)) ≡⟨ cong reverse (reverse-++ x xs) ⟩
  reverse (reverse xs ++ [ x ]) ≡⟨ reverse-++-distrib (reverse xs) [ x ] ⟩
  reverse [ x ] ++ reverse (reverse xs) ≡⟨ cong (_++ reverse (reverse xs)) (reverse-[x] x) ⟩
  [ x ] ++ reverse (reverse xs) ≡⟨ cong ([ x ] ++_) (reverse-involutive xs) ⟩
  x ∷ xs ∎

hypothesis: For all natural numbers n, the successor is not zero
proof:
suc≢zero : ∀ {n} → suc n ≡ 0 → ⊥
suc≢zero ()

hypothesis: For all natural numbers a and b, a plus the successor of b equals the successor of a plus b
proof:
+-suc : ∀ a b → a + suc b ≡ suc (a + b)
+-suc zero b = refl
+-suc (suc a) b = cong suc (+-suc a b)

hypothesis: If a equals b then f of a equals f of b
proof:
cong-app : ∀ {A B : Set} {a b : A} (f : A → B) → a ≡ b → f a ≡ f b
cong-app f refl = refl

hypothesis: For all natural numbers a, b, c, a plus b plus c equals a plus b plus c
proof:
+-assoc : ∀ a b c → (a + b) + c ≡ a + (b + c)
+-assoc zero b c = refl
+-assoc (suc a) b c = cong suc (+-assoc a b c)

hypothesis: For all booleans b, b or false equals b
proof:
∨-identityʳ : ∀ b → b ∨ false ≡ b
∨-identityʳ true = refl
∨-identityʳ false = refl

hypothesis: For all natural numbers a and b, a times b equals b times a
proof:
*-comm : ∀ a b → a * b ≡ b * a
*-comm zero b = sym (*-zeroʳ b)
*-comm (suc a) b = begin
  suc a * b   ≡⟨ refl ⟩
  b + a * b   ≡⟨ cong (b +_) (*-comm a b) ⟩
  b + b * a   ≡⟨ sym (*-suc b a) ⟩
  b * suc a   ∎

hypothesis: For all lists l, the length of l is greater than or equal to zero
proof:
length-nonneg : ∀ {A : Set} (xs : List A) → length xs ≥ 0
length-nonneg [] = z≤n
length-nonneg (x ∷ xs) = s≤s (length-nonneg xs)

hypothesis: If a number is divisible by 4, it is divisible by 2
proof:
div4-div2 : ∀ n → n mod 4 ≡ 0 → n mod 2 ≡ 0
div4-div2 n h = begin
  n mod 2 ≡⟨ mod-cong n (m*n≡0⇒m≡0 2 (begin
    2 * (n mod 2) ≡⟨ *-distribˡ-mod 2 n 2 ⟩
    (2 * n) mod 4 ≡⟨ cong (_mod 4) (sym (*-comm n 2)) ⟩
    (n * 2) mod 4 ≡⟨ *-mod-cong n 2 4 h ⟩
    0 ∎)) ⟩
  0 ∎

hypothesis: For all natural numbers a and b, the minimum is less than or equal to a
proof:
min-≤ˡ : ∀ a b → min a b ≤ a
min-≤ˡ zero b = z≤n
min-≤ˡ (suc a) zero = z≤n
min-≤ˡ (suc a) (suc b) = s≤s (min-≤ˡ a b)

hypothesis: For all natural numbers a, a squared is non-negative
proof:
sq-nonneg : ∀ a → a * a ≥ 0
sq-nonneg a = ≤-reflexive (begin
  0 ≡⟨ sym (*-zeroʳ a) ⟩
  a * 0 ≤⟨ *-monoʳ-≤ a z≤n ⟩
  a * a ∎)

hypothesis: For all lists a and b, the length of a appended with b equals the sum of their lengths
proof:
length-++ : ∀ {A : Set} (xs ys : List A) → length (xs ++ ys) ≡ length xs + length ys
length-++ [] ys = refl
length-++ (x ∷ xs) ys = cong suc (length-++ xs ys)

hypothesis: For all booleans a, a and true equals a
proof:
∧-identityʳ : ∀ a → a ∧ true ≡ a
∧-identityʳ true = refl
∧-identityʳ false = refl

hypothesis: For all natural numbers a and b, if a equals b then a plus c equals b plus c
proof:
+-congˡ : ∀ {a b} c → a ≡ b → a + c ≡ b + c
+-congˡ c refl = refl

hypothesis: For all natural numbers n, 2 to the power n is greater than zero
If a divides b then a divides b times c
proof:
∣-⇒∣-* : ∀ {a b c} → a ∣ b → a ∣ b * c
∣-⇒∣-* (divides q eq) = divides (q * c) (begin
  a * (q * c) ≡⟨ sym (*-assoc a q c) ⟩
  (a * q) * c ≡⟨ cong (_* c) eq ⟩
  b * c ∎)

hypothesis: For all lists l, map of identity on l equals l
proof:
map-id : ∀ {A : Set} (xs : List A) → map id xs ≡ xs
map-id [] = refl
map-id (x ∷ xs) = cong (x ∷_) (map-id xs)

hypothesis: For all natural numbers a, b, c, a times b plus c equals a times b plus a times c
proof:
*-distrib-+ : ∀ a b c → a * (b + c) ≡ a * b + a * c
*-distrib-+ zero b c = refl
*-distrib-+ (suc a) b c = begin
  suc a * (b + c) ≡⟨ refl ⟩
  (b + c) + a * (b + c) ≡⟨ cong ((b + c) +_) (*-distrib-+ a b c) ⟩
  (b + c) + (a * b + a * c) ≡⟨ +-assoc b c (a * b + a * c) ⟩
  b + (c + (a * b + a * c)) ≡⟨ cong (b +_) (sym (+-assoc c (a * b) (a * c))) ⟩
  b + ((c + a * b) + a * c) ≡⟨ cong (λ x → b + (x + a * c)) (+-comm c (a * b)) ⟩
  b + ((a * b + c) + a * c) ≡⟨ cong (b +_) (+-assoc (a * b) c (a * c)) ⟩
  b + (a * b + (c + a * c)) ≡⟨ sym (+-assoc b (a * b) (c + a * c)) ⟩
  (b + a * b) + (c + a * c) ≡⟨ refl ⟩
  suc a * b + suc a * c ∎

hypothesis: For all natural numbers a and b, a plus b is greater than or equal to a
proof:
m≤m+n : ∀ m n → m ≤ m + n
m≤m+n zero n = z≤n
m≤m+n (suc m) n = s≤s (m≤m+n m n)

hypothesis: For all booleans a and b, a or b equals b or a
proof:
∨-comm : ∀ a b → a ∨ b ≡ b ∨ a
∨-comm true true = refl
∨-comm true false = refl
∨-comm false true = refl
∨-comm false false = refl

hypothesis: For all natural numbers a, b, c, a times b times c equals a times b times c
proof:
*-assoc : ∀ a b c → (a * b) * c ≡ a * (b * c)
*-assoc zero b c = refl
*-assoc (suc a) b c = begin
  suc a * b * c ≡⟨ refl ⟩
  (b + a * b) * c ≡⟨ *-distrib-+ b (a * b) c ⟩
  b * c + a * b * c ≡⟨ cong (b * c +_) (*-assoc a b c) ⟩
  b * c + a * (b * c) ≡⟨ refl ⟩
  suc a * (b * c) ∎

hypothesis: For all natural numbers n, n is less than or equal to n
proof:
≤-refl : ∀ {n} → n ≤ n
≤-refl {zero} = z≤n
≤-refl {suc n} = s≤s ≤-refl

hypothesis: If a is less than b then a is less than or equal to b
For all natural numbers n, n is less than n plus one
proof:
n<sucn : ∀ n → n < suc n
n<sucn n = s≤s ≤-refl

hypothesis: For all lists a and b, map of f over a appended with b equals map of f over a appended with map of f over b
proof:
map-++ : ∀ {A B : Set} (f : A → B) (xs ys : List A) → map f (xs ++ ys) ≡ map f xs ++ map f ys
map-++ f [] ys = refl
map-++ f (x ∷ xs) ys = cong (f x ∷_) (map-++ f xs ys)

hypothesis: For all natural numbers a, the factorial of a is positive
proof:
fact-pos : ∀ a → fact a > 0
fact-pos zero = s≤s z≤n
fact-pos (suc a) = begin
  0 <⟨ fact-pos a ⟩
  fact a ≤⟨ m≤m+n (fact a) (a * fact a) ⟩
  fact a + a * fact a ≡⟨ refl ⟩
  fact (suc a) ∎

hypothesis: For all natural numbers n, zero to the power of n is zero for positive n
For all booleans b, b or not b equals true
proof:
∨-neg : ∀ b → b ∨ not b ≡ true
∨-neg true = refl
∨-neg false = refl

hypothesis: For all lists l, the length of the reverse equals the length
proof:
length-reverse : ∀ {A : Set} (xs : List A) → length (reverse xs) ≡ length xs
length-reverse [] = refl
length-reverse (x ∷ xs) = begin
  length (reverse (x ∷ xs)) ≡⟨ cong length (reverse-++ x xs) ⟩
  length (reverse xs ++ [ x ]) ≡⟨ length-++ (reverse xs) [ x ] ⟩
  length (reverse xs) + 1 ≡⟨ cong (_+ 1) (length-reverse xs) ⟩
  length xs + 1 ≡⟨ +-comm (length xs) 1 ⟩
  1 + length xs ≡⟨ refl ⟩
  length (x ∷ xs) ∎

hypothesis: If a is less than b then a plus one is less than or equal to b
proof:
<⇒suc≤ : ∀ {a b} → a < b → suc a ≤ b
<⇒suc≤ (s≤s a≤b) = s≤s a≤b

hypothesis: For all natural numbers a, b, c, if a equals b then a times c equals b times c
proof:
*-congˡ : ∀ {a b} c → a ≡ b → a * c ≡ b * c
*-congˡ c refl = refl

hypothesis: For all lists l, the length of l is zero if and only if l is empty
proof:
length≡0⇒[] : ∀ {A : Set} {xs : List A} → length xs ≡ 0 → xs ≡ []
length≡0⇒[] {xs = []} _ = refl
length≡0⇒[] {xs = x ∷ xs} ()

hypothesis: For all natural numbers n, if n is even then n mod 2 equals 0
proof:
even⇒mod2≡0 : ∀ n → Even n → n mod 2 ≡ 0
even⇒mod2≡0 n (even p q eq) = begin
  n mod 2 ≡⟨ cong (_mod 2) eq ⟩
  (2 * p) mod 2 ≡⟨ *-mod 2 p 2 ⟩
  0 ∎

hypothesis: For all natural numbers a, a times the successor of b equals a times b plus a
proof:
*-suc : ∀ a b → a * suc b ≡ a * b + a
*-suc zero b = refl
*-suc (suc a) b = begin
  suc a * suc b ≡⟨ refl ⟩
  suc b + a * suc b ≡⟨ cong (suc b +_) (*-suc a b) ⟩
  suc b + (a * b + a) ≡⟨ +-assoc (suc b) (a * b) a ⟩
  (suc b + a * b) + a ≡⟨ cong (_+ a) (+-suc b (a * b)) ⟩
  suc (b + a * b) + a ≡⟨ refl ⟩
  suc (suc a * b) + a ≡⟨ refl ⟩
  suc a * b + suc a ∎

hypothesis: For all lists l, l appended with empty list equals l
proof:
++-identityʳ : ∀ {A : Set} (xs : List A) → xs ++ [] ≡ xs
++-identityʳ [] = refl
++-identityʳ (x ∷ xs) = cong (x ∷_) (++-identityʳ xs)

hypothesis: For all natural numbers a and b, a plus b equals a plus b
proof:
+-comm-simple : ∀ a b → a + b ≡ b + a
+-comm-simple = add-comm
